title: Wound cover apparatus and related methods of use
abstract: A wound therapy apparatus is disclosed herein that, in various aspects, includes a wound interface that forms an enclosed space over the wound bed that is fluid-tight in order to maintain a pressure p0 within the enclosed space that differs from the ambient pressure pamb. A dressing engaged with the wound interface may contact the wound bed, and the dressing may include a hydrophobic material, hydrophobic material, or a distal layer comprised of silicone having fenestrations therein, in various aspects. The enclosed space may include gas communicated into the enclosed space having an O2 concentration greater than the O2 concentration of atmospheric air, and the pressure within the enclosed space may be varied over a pressure range. Related methods of use of the wound therapy apparatus are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS The invention claimed is : 1. A wound therapy apparatus, comprising: a wound interface secured to a skin surface around a wound bed to form an enclosed space over the wound bed that is fluid-tight; a dressing engaged with the wound interface within the enclosed space; a lumen passing through the wound interface that fluidly communicates with the enclosed space; and gas having an O2 concentration greater than that of atmospheric air, the gas is sequentially input into the enclosed space and withdrawn from the enclosed space via the lumen to vary the pressure po within the enclosed space over a pressure cycle having pressure range p mm â¤ poâ¤ p ma x where p mm is a minimum pressure during the pressure cycle and p ma x is a maximum pressure during the pressure cycle. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, the dressing comprises a distal layer comprised of silicone, a distal side of the distal layer contacts the wound bed; and fenestrations disposed in the distal layer that fluidly communicate between the distal side and a proximal side of the distal layer. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a second lumen passing through the wound interface that fluidly communicates with the enclosed space; and liquid input into the enclosed space via the lumen and withdrawn from the enclosed space via the second lumen. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein p ma x is within the range of from about -15 mm Hg to about + 10 mm Hg with respect to ambient pressure p am b. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein p ma x is within the range of from about -10 mm Hg to about +40 mm Hg with respect to ambient pressure p am b. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the minimum pressure p mm within the range of from about - 40 mm Hg to about -150 mm with respect to ambient pressure p am b. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, the dressing comprises medicament for delivery to the wound bed. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a pressure relief valve disposed about the wound interface to limit the pressure po within the enclosed space to less than a limiting pressure pi. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: an injection port functionally connected to the wound interface to allow delivery of medicament into the enclosed space. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: connectors disposed about the wound interface for electrical communication with the wound interface to generate an electromagnetic field about the wound bed. 11. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a filter disposed between the dressing and a suction source to limit the flow of exudate towards the suction source, the suction source provides suction to the lumen. 12. A wound therapy apparatus, comprising: a wound interface securable to a skin surface around a wound bed to form an enclosed space over the wound bed that is fluid-tight in order to maintain a pressure po within the enclosed space that differs from the ambient pressure pamb; a dressing engaged with the wound interface within the enclosed space, the dressing comprising at least a distal layer comprised, at least in part, of silicone, a distal side of the distal layer to contact the wound bed when the wound interface is secured; and fenestrations disposed in the distal layer that fluidly communicate between the distal side and a proximal side of the distal layer. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: 

 gas within the enclosed space having an O2 concentration greater than the O2 concentration of atmospheric air. 14. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: a port disposed about the wound interface with a lumen passing through the port that fluidly communicates with the enclosed space. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, further comprising: a spacer disposed between the dressing and the lumen within the enclosed space to define a plenum in fluid communication with the lumen and a proximal side of the dressing. 16. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: a hydrophobic material in cooperation with the proximal side of the distal layer to transfer exudate away from the proximal side of the distal layer and hydrophilic material in cooperation with the hydrophilic material to transfer exudate away from the hydrophobic material. 17. The apparatus of claim 12, the dressing further comprises : a second layer proximal of the distal layer, the second layer comprises a medicament to be released to the wound bed. 18. The apparatus of claim 12, the dressing further comprises : a second layer proximal of the distal layer, the second layer defines channels to communicate fluids in the second layer. 19. A wound therapy apparatus, comprising: a wound interface secured to a skin surface around a wound bed to form an enclosed space over the wound bed that is fluid-tight in order to maintain a pressure po within the enclosed space that differs from the ambient pressure p am b; a dressing engaged with the wound interface to contact the wound bed; and gas within the enclosed space having an O2 concentration greater than atmospheric air, there being no gas flow into the enclosed space or out of the enclosed space. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, further comprising: a hydrophobic material that forms a portion of the dressing, the hydrophobic material transfers exudate from the wound bed; and a hydrophilic material in fluid cooperation with the hydrophobic material to transfer exudate from the hydrophobic material.
